attributes.description: Alerts when `rocksdb_read_amplification` on a store exceeds 50, indicating each logical read is costing many physical reads. This is a strong signal of an unhealthy LSM tree.
attributes.name: [CockroachDB OTel] LSM read amplification high

investigation_guide.blob:
## CockroachDB LSM read amplification high

### What fired
`rocksdb_read_amplification` on one or more stores exceeded 50 over the evaluation window. In a healthy cluster this metric should sit in the single digits.

### Why it matters
Read amplification is the average number of physical disk reads required per logical read. Elevated values mean the LSM tree is bloated (usually L0/L6 imbalance) and all reads are paying the cost. Sustained values above 50 are documented as "unhealthy LSM".

### Triage
1. On the Storage dashboard, correlate with `storage_l0_sublevels`, `storage_l0_num_files`, `rocksdb_compactions` rate, and `admission_io_overload`.
2. Check disk health: `sys_host_disk_iopsinprogress`, provider IOPS metrics.
3. Verify compactions aren't CPU-starved (`sys_cpu_combined_percent_normalized`, `sys_runnable_goroutines_per_cpu`).

### Remediation
- Throttle writes until compactions recover.
- Provision more IOPS or CPU if compactions cannot keep up with steady-state load.
- As a last resort, consider a manual compaction (advanced — coordinate with support).

### Tuning
- `> 50` for 15 minutes is the documented unhealthy-LSM signal. Lower for early warning or raise if your workload tolerates higher amplification.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-cockroachdb.otel-default*
| WHERE service.name == "cockroachdb"
  AND rocksdb_read_amplification IS NOT NULL
  AND rocksdb_read_amplification > 50
// > 50 for sustained period indicates unhealthy LSM
| WHERE attributes.node_id IS NOT NULL AND attributes.store IS NOT NULL
| STATS amplification = MAX(rocksdb_read_amplification) BY attributes.node_id, host.name
| SORT amplification DESC
